Stem: The text describes a generative large-language model that is pretrained on protein sequences and fine-tuned on about 1,000 major ampullate spidroin (MaSp) sequences with associated fiber-level mechanical properties. The model is used in an end-to-end forward and inverse generative approach to generate novel spidroin sequences conditioned on target mechanical property sets. Performance and self-consistency are assessed using novelty checks and protein type classification via BLAST, property evaluation against similar sequences, comparison of resulting molecular structures, and sequence motif analyses. Target property sets include common, rare, and non-existent elastic modulus–toughness pairs, with remaining property values set to medians.
Q1: What conditional relationship between input mechanical property sets (including common, rare, and non-existent elastic modulus–toughness pairs) and the model’s outputs (generated MaSp-like sequences and predicted fiber-level properties) is being claimed, and how is this relationship evaluated across the different assessment steps described?
Q2: Using only the concepts stated in the text, why should fine-tuning a pretrained protein-sequence model on MaSp sequences with associated fiber-level properties enable both (i) generation of novel spidroin sequences and (ii) exploration of sequence–property relationships for elastic modulus, strength, toughness, and failure strain?
Q3: The text mentions a “self-consistency” evaluation using five selected property sets; operationally define what would count as evidence of self-consistency using the listed checks (BLAST novelty/classification, property evaluation vs similar sequences, folding configuration comparison, motif analysis), and specify at least two measurable outcomes implied by these checks.
Q4: Propose one falsifiable follow-up test, grounded in the described workflow, that would challenge the interpretation that the model can generate sequences with “property combinations that do not exist in nature”; state what you would expect to observe for sequences generated from an existing elastic modulus–toughness pair versus the non-existent elevated pair (set 4) under your proposed test.
Q5: What are the exact numerical values of all eight dimensions in the “8D mechanical property sets” used as generation inputs (including the non-E/toughness dimensions) for each of the five selected sets in Table 1?
Q6: If the model can generate silk sequences with mechanical-property combinations that do not exist in nature, what hypothesis would you propose—anchored in the paper’s emphasis on sequence motifs and folding configurations—about why such combinations are absent naturally, and how would you prioritize the next dataset expansion or analysis step to distinguish biological absence from sampling limitations in the Silkome-derived MaSp dataset?
